headline: Hacking Voice AI 👨‍💻
about: I build production voice AI systems on Pipecat and LiveKit. Full SaaS products, custom infrastructure, and migrations off managed platforms like Vapi and Retell.  The problem is that building voice AI on open-source frameworks is genuinely difficult to get right. You're figuring out which STT/LLM/TTS combinations work, where to place your servers for optimal latency, debugging why turn detection keeps interrupting mid-sentence, and learning what actually works at scale versus what looks good in demos.  I've been doing this for nearly two years. I've debugged the failures, optimised the pipelines, and built the boilerplate.  I also write about AI-assisted development and software security. If you've found me through one of those posts, that's the same engineering mindset applied to different problems.  Tech stack: Python, Next.js, TypeScript, Supabase, Vercel, with Pipecat or LiveKit depending on your needs.  I offer consultancy for architecture and migration planning, or implementation where I build it and hand it over. Sometimes both.  If you're building voice AI and want to talk through your approach, get in touch.

headline: Founder @ intotheopen: Solving Ineffective Content Strategy with AI | LLDC/Elevate Board Member: Shaping & Uplifting Communities and Futures | Local Champions CIC Project Manager | Author
about: I’m Emmanuel Lawal, an entrepreneur, storyteller, and builder passionate about transforming ideas into ventures that create real-world impact.  I’m the Founder of intotheopen, an AI-native content intelligence platform for communications and marketing agencies. Our mission is simple: to solve the $90B global problem of ineffective content strategy by giving teams the foresight to engineer campaigns that deliver measurable results. From concept to securing enterprise contracts, building a strong pipeline of B2B SaaS clients, intotheopen is primed to become a trusted partner for all future-focused communications and marketing agencies.  Beyond intotheopen, I serve as a Project Manager for Local Champions CIC, where I lead the Young Entrepreneurs Alliance (YEA!) accelerator program. Through YEA!, I work with 18–30-year-old founders to refine their ideas, secure funding, and build sustainable businesses. I’ve designed and delivered programs in partnership with organisations such as Peabody and London Legacy Development Corporation (LLDC), helping emerging talent access networks, mentorship, and opportunities that can change their trajectories.  I'm also a long-standing member of the LLDC Elevate board, where I provide consultancy on major regeneration projects, including East Bank, the National Gallery’s NG200, and housing developments that are shaping the future of East London.  At the heart of all my work is a commitment to storytelling and strategy. From writing and publishing to consulting and leading teams, I believe the most powerful ventures are built when creativity, clarity, and purpose meet execution.  I thrive at the intersection of entrepreneurship, innovation, and community impact, whether building SaaS products, running accelerator programs, or supporting regeneration projects.  If you’re working on something aligned with B2B SaaS, MarTech, Future of Work, or social impact entrepreneurship, I’d love to connect.

headline: GTM Leader | Partnerships & Enterprise Sales | Transforming Complex Tech into Narrative-Driven Growth for AI Cloud Infrastructure Platforms
about: I’ve spent my career at the intersection of complex infrastructure and human impact, helping global enterprises and high-growth startups turn "what’s next" into measurable growth.  From managing multi-million dollar portfolios for icons like Facebook and Salesforce at Dell, to building the 0-to-1 global GTM engine for an AI-native platform at Koyeb, my work is defined by a single objective: architecting the narratives that make innovation accessible. I believe the biggest barrier to progress isn’t a lack of technology—it’s a lack of translation; it's all about leaning into storytelling!  What I bring to the table: -The Architecture of Trust: I don’t just close deals; I build ecosystems. My approach is rooted in the belief that the strongest partnerships are forged through long-term reliability rather than short-term transactions. Whether navigating 12-month enterprise cycles or designing programs at Care.com, I prioritize the deep, consultative relationships that allow for consistent account growth and mutual success. -The Innovation Lens: My background is a mix of Silicon Valley rigor and creativity. This allows me to see trigger events others tend to miss, identifying exactly where a disruptive technology meets a partner’s core business challenge. -Operational Grit: I’m a disciplined strategist who values the data. I thrive on testing, tracking, and iterating on GTM strategies to ensure commercial promises are backed by execution excellence.  The "Why": As an MBA and certified coach, I enjoy the ambiguous middle, the space where a new idea needs a clear path to market. I’m passionate about bridging the gap between technical and business impact, and staying two steps ahead of industry trends so my partners and customers aren't just reacting to the future, but building it together.

headline: Connector | Controlled access to enterprise buying motion
about: I operate as a Connector for a small number of high-ticket B2B companies.  Enterprise buying decisions don’t begin when outreach starts. They form internally — through leadership shifts, budget reallocations, and strategic initiatives — long before they become visible.  I step in at that moment.  Access is released into live buying motion, when intent already exists and stakeholders are aligned.  There is no volume layer. No sequences. No pipeline padding.  Each introduction is deliberate, contextual, and positioned inside an active evaluation — where conversations carry weight.  The result is fewer conversations, higher leverage, and materially stronger deal outcomes.  I work with a limited number of partners. If timing and positioning aren’t aligned, nothing moves.

headline: Simplifying remote work around the world 👀 | Founder @ Badge 📱| Incubated @ Unicorn Factory Lisboa 🦄 | EuroTech Fellow 🇪🇺
about: Not your typical master’s student… or your typical founder 🤪  Hey, I'm Julia – a startup founder, marketing enthusiast, communication nerd, world traveler, pet lover, and wannabe food critic 👋  To know a little bit about me, let's dive into the six places in this world that have my ❤️:  🇧🇷: I am a proud "paulista" – born and raised in the chaos of the huge city of São Paulo.  🇵🇹: Lisbon is what I like to call my second home. It’s where I spend most of my free time. Oh, and let’s not forget the pastéis de nata!  🇳🇱: I’m lucky to have lived in the beautiful city of Amsterdam. There, I completed my Bachelor's in Communication Science and Minor in Managing Strategy and Marketing at the UvA. It’s also where I founded my startup, Badge – a platform helping remote workers find laptop-friendly spots across the city. Curious? Head over to Badge’s LinkedIn or drop me a message.  🇸🇬: The National University of Singapore, ranked #11 in the 2023 QS World Rankings, was my choice for a semester abroad. I was accepted and ranked #1, and got the chance to live some of my most amazing experiences yet while exploring 8 new countries throughout Southeast Asia.  🇦🇹: Then comes Vienna, where I completed the first year of my Master’s in Marketing at WU – but most of my time is spent building Badge and bringing new ideas to life.  🇮🇹: And now I’m based in Milan, where I’m completing my Masters of Science in Marketing Management at Bocconi. Here, I’m a full time student and full time founder - no time to choose!  What’s next? I don’t believe in boring, and I’m on a quest to make everything I’m a part of just a little bit more exciting. So, let’s connect and innovate together!

headline: Founder @ The Chainsmokers + Mantis Venture Capital | Early-Stage Investor | Innovation, Technology & Culture
about: Born in New York City, I’ve always had a deep passion for music that manifested itself in unexpected ways. I wasn't interested in playing an instrument or singing at an early age, I was more interested in art and business.   Nevertheless, I was always drawn to music in strange ways: I loved exploring the intersection between different genres, technologies and sounds. I constantly tinkered with stereo systems, mixing and matching tracks to create my own unique playlists and remixes. Of course, it was 1998 so my options were limited.  At the time, I didn't necessarily see music as a career path - or even consider it. I didn't know any successful musicians and there were certainly none in my family. However, in the years to come, I would learn music was something I wanted to pursue in some form or another.  In 2012, I met Drew Taggart, and The Chainsmokers were born.  Years of long days producing in our tiny studio apartment in NYC and playing underground clubs at night to make ends meet, combined with Drew's experience as a producer, eventually proved to be a winning formula.  As The Chainsmokers began to take off, I also became interested in investing and early-stage startups. I took immense pleasure in, and was incredibly inspired by, working with founders who were excited to create disruption with their ambitious ideas.   I saw firsthand how technology was revolutionizing the music industry and realized that I wanted to be a part of that change, not just in music, but across tech, finance, CPG, and more. I knew we had more to offer early-stage companies than what people might assume.  In 2019, I founded the venture firm Mantis VC alongside Drew, Jeffrey Evans, and Milan Koch.  As an entrepreneur who created a music industry enterprise, I knew that bringing that same energy and experience to other founders could lead to huge things. In addition to being a creator at my core, I am a leader, team builder, and growth hacker, and I love using those skills to serve our founder network within my angel and VC portfolio.  As I look to the future, I get extremely excited to continue growing Mantis VC and supporting the next generation of entrepreneurs and innovators.  More fun stuff about me - I love:  - Dogs (give Mooshu a follow on IG, yeah? @mooshuthegoldendumpling) - Terrible action movies - Skiing, but not too fast because I tour too much - Philanthropy

about: Henreich Ecija is a dedicated healthcare innovator with over a decade of experience in dentistry and dental technology. A GDC-registered dental care professional since 2010 and a board-certified dentist under the Professional Regulation Commission (PRC) since 2005, Henreich combines clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology to advance global healthcare outcomes. His commitment to patient safety is reflected in his groundbreaking, patent-pending solution, designed to revolutionise the worldwide standard in dental implant detection and enhance patient care.  He holds a Doctor of Dental Medicine from Centro Escolar University, an HND in Dental Technology with specialization in Dental Implants and Fixed-Prosthodontics from Edinburgh Telford College, and a Postgraduate Masters Diploma in Occupational Health, Safety, and Environmental Management from Portsmouth University. He is also a Technical Working Committee member of Dentistry at ISO   Henreich’s dissertation on Integrated Quality Management Systems (QMS), Environmental Management Systems (EMS), and Health and Safety Management Systems (HSMS) highlights his expertise in compliance strategies, operational efficiency, and regulatory adherence within healthcare frameworks. With a focus on improving organisational performance and safety, Henreich remains dedicated to fostering innovations that can have a tangible, positive impact on patient outcomes and global healthcare standards.  As the founder of a patent-pending  dental technology, Henreich is leading the development of a novel solution designed to support safer and more efficient implant procedures. The innovation currently under global PCT application aims to assist dental professionals in accurately verifying implant components in clinical and laboratory settings. By streamlining an often complex and error-prone task, this solution helps enhance patient care.  Henreich is always open to connecting with forward-thinking professionals and organisations passionate about advancing healthcare through innovation.

headline: Co Founder, Prime Health Technologies |   Building the Operating System for Population Health, Longevity, and Prevention
about: I am a scientist and entrepreneur focused on building health systems that work at scale.  Despite unprecedented advances in diagnostics, wearables, and medical science, global health outcomes continue to decline. The missing layer is not data or treatment. It is execution. Most health systems fail in the space between insight and daily behavior.  My work sits at that intersection.  I have co founded and led multiple ventures spanning clinical research, elite performance, consumer wellness, and now national scale health infrastructure.  🔬 ASPI (Applied Science & Performance Institute) ASPI is a clinical research and performance institute translating cutting edge science into real world applications across health, longevity, beauty, and elite sport. We partner with professional teams, brands, and institutions to validate interventions, reduce injury risk, and improve human performance through rigorous research.  🌍 Prime Health Technologies Prime Health is building the operating system for population health. We partner with governments and insurers to turn diagnostics, wearables, and behavioral data into personalized daily actions delivered at national scale. The focus is prevention, metabolic health, and measurable outcomes, not episodic care or standalone apps.  🚀 Pro2col Health Pro2col is a personalized wellness platform focused on precision protocols and digital diagnostics. It was acquired by Herbalife as part of their transition toward science driven, personalized health solutions.  Across all of these efforts, the thesis is consistent: Health does not fail because of a lack of science. It fails because there is no system that owns daily execution.  I am passionate about working with governments, insurers, investors, and builders who believe the future of healthcare is proactive, personalized, and behavior first.  Let’s connect if you are building, funding, or enabling the next generation of health and longevity infrastructure.

headline: Helping Tech Companies Ship Faster | Founder @Cohort, Cofounder @Egen(exited)
about: I grew up in India believing that the right room changes everything.  Not the fanciest room. Not the most powerful one. The right one.  The one where the right people sit across from each other at exactly the right moment — and something gets built that wouldn't have existed otherwise.  That belief became a career. Fifteen years. Three companies. One through line.  𝗖𝗼𝗵𝗼𝗿𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝗿𝗱. 𝗜𝘁 i𝘀 𝗮𝗹𝘀𝗼 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗼𝗻𝗲 𝗜 𝗰𝗼𝘂𝗹𝗱𝗻'𝘁 𝗵𝗮𝘃𝗲 𝗯𝘂𝗶𝗹𝘁 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵𝗼𝘂𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗳𝗶𝗿𝘀𝘁 𝘁𝘄𝗼 𝗯𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗸𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗺𝗲 𝗶𝗻 𝗲𝘅𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗹𝘆 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗿𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁 𝗽𝗹𝗮𝗰𝗲𝘀.  At Egen, we built something real. A 1,000+person engineering org. Global AI platforms. Enterprise clients who trusted us with their most critical infrastructure.  What the outside world saw was the scale. What we lived was the cost of building it.  Hiring nearly broke us.  Not because we were careless. Because the system was broken. Every search took months. Our best engineers disappeared into interview loops. Every bad hire cost us not just money — it cost us momentum.  We had advantages most founders never get. Brand. Networks. Resources.  The founder scaling from 10 to 50 engineers right now has none of that. They are making the most consequential hiring decisions of their company's life with a spreadsheet and an HR generalist who has never evaluated a distributed systems engineer.  That is the problem I couldn't stop thinking about after Egen.  𝟭𝟱 𝘆𝗲𝗮𝗿𝘀. 𝗧𝗵𝗿𝗲𝗲 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗮𝗻𝗶𝗲𝘀. 𝗢𝗻𝗲 𝗼𝗯𝘀𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻.  𝗘𝗴𝗲𝗻 — 1,000+ person engineering org. Global AI platforms. World-class teams powering Chicago's category-defining unicorns. Successful exit to Recognize.  𝟮,𝟬𝟬𝟬+ 𝗲𝗻𝗴𝗶𝗻𝗲𝗲𝗿𝘀 𝗲𝘃𝗮𝗹𝘂𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗱, 𝗵𝗶𝗿𝗲𝗱, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗼𝗻𝗯𝗼𝗮𝗿𝗱𝗲𝗱.  𝗖𝗼𝗵𝗼𝗿𝘁 is an AI-native talent platform built for one purpose: finding the engineers your company needs, in days not months.  The talent infrastructure scaling companies should have had from the beginning. 1M+ pre-vetted engineers. A proprietary methodology built from 2,000 real hires. No recruiters. No endless pipelines.  𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗺𝗶𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻: Collapse the false choice between speed and quality.  𝗦𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗲𝘀 𝗔–𝗗 𝗳𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗿 𝗹𝗼𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗲𝗻𝗴𝗶𝗻𝗲𝗲𝗿𝘀 𝘁𝗼 𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗿𝘃𝗶𝗲𝘄 𝗹𝗼𝗼𝗽𝘀 → send me a message.  𝗢𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗼𝗿, 𝗶𝗻𝘃𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿, 𝗼𝗿 𝘁𝗲𝗰𝗵𝗻𝗶𝗰𝗮𝗹 𝗹𝗲𝗮𝗱𝗲𝗿 → follow along.  𝗘𝗻𝗴𝗶𝗻𝗲𝗲𝗿 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗱𝘆 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝘆𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗻𝗲𝘅𝘁 𝗿𝗼𝗹𝗲 → the network is open.  The infrastructure exists. The question is whether your company uses it before your next competitor does.

headline: Editor-in-Chief, Stockholm Valley
about: Welcome to my LinkedIn page - open some bubbly, put on L’impératrice & send me a message if you will.  I believe great stories fuel innovation, anyone at any age can move an industry, and that the future is in collaboration through globalised markets.  My name is Daniel, a 20 y/o Swede who is currently studying a BA in Business & Management at Durham University. I began my education at a state-school in the UK called St John the Baptist where I eventually became Head Boy, co-leading with a team of 22 students day-to-day. Once finished, I moved to Stockholm for a year where I conducted research alongside postdoctorals on Brain-Computer Interfaces at the Stockholm School of Economics and Sweden's House of Innovation, presenting my findings to undergraduates, postgraduates, faculty and SSE Executive Education.  Throughout my educational journey, I underwent immense professional, academic and personal development: becoming the youngest in Europe ever to present a university seminar, starting Stockholm Valley which became the no.1 publication of its kind (100k monthly circulation) in a month, and regularly liaising with VCs and founders across Europe and North America. Despite being "young," all of these opportunities came through three desires: a desire to ask questions, a desire to learn and a desire to engage.  My focus is on embracing the seismic socio-economic changes of technological developments and living in a digital age. With an international upbringing, I began to notice how slow "first-world" countries are at embracing change, the lack of collaboration under survivalist mindsets like "eat or get eaten," and the apparent preference of uniformity over originality. That's why today, through my journalism and the ideas I put out, I hope to shed light on how originality is more traditional than uniformity, that societies where all have access to unlimited education should scrap strict qualitative biases, and how the world would be a more peaceful place if sharing was the norm over secrecy.  If you're someone who shares an interest in the aforementioned: I would love to discuss this further and exchange thoughts, regardless if you agree with my views or not. Connect with me and send a message, the more personality the better.  (P.S. If you take one thing away, the best economy is under sufficiency economy theory: build for GOOD and LONGEVITY, not for a quick pay-check... but if you can do both, you're a prodigy and I want to write about you).

Through his lyrics and melodies, Alex Hracan invites listeners on a journey of self-discovery and connection with the world around them. His songs are not just musical compositions; they are windows into the soul, reflecting the universal themes of love, loss, and the quest for meaning. Whether he's crafting lines of code or crafting melodies, he remains committed to pushing the boundaries of what is possible and inspiring others to do the same.  In a world that often feels disconnected from nature, Alex Hracan serves as a reminder of the profound beauty and importance of our natural surroundings. Through his music, he invites us to reconnect with the world around us, to listen to its whispers, and to find solace in its embrace. At automate rev.ops., I work on workflows that turn LinkedIn into a reliable revenue channel for sales teams.  Not vanity metrics. Not random connections. Real conversations, triggered at the right moment, with the right context.  Here’s the kind of work I focus on:  → LinkedIn workflows driven by signals and timing, not volume → Clay automations that book meetings while reps focus on closing → Content and engagement systems that warm prospects before outreach → Turning LinkedIn from a manual task into a repeatable pipeline engine  What if your LinkedIn outreach didn’t depend on daily effort? What if the system kept running even when your team wasn’t?  That’s exactly what we build.  🚀 If LinkedIn is part of your GTM but feels inconsistent or hard to scale, let’s talk. You can also check the featured section to get practical workflows delivered weekly.

headline: 25+ Years Automotive Innovation @Bosch | Launching MindVenture (AI + Blockchain) | Helping Tech Builders & Investors Unlock Scalable Growth & Smart Automation | Seeking Builders, VCs, and Tech Talent | 10+ Patents
about: Level up your business by transformative AI technology.  Starting my technical studies and professional life in the middle of the computer age and digital economy, the technology seemed to have a linear evolution.  With 25+ years at Bosch leading innovation in ADAS, embedded systems, and automotive software, I know how to build systems that scale — from architecture to execution.  At Bosch, I led:  - Global teams of 150+ engineers - Architecture of centralized compute platforms for autonomous driving - Development of scalable ADAS middleware and integration frameworks - Filing of 10+ patents across embedded software and development tools  Also AI has been one of many technology elements in our technology stack since several years. But with the advent of ChatGPT everything changed, the product architecture (e.g. Vision-Language-Models), but also the product development (e.g. Agentic Workflows). At the same time, this technological revolution has been overlayed with massive structural adaptions in the automotive industry as well as with geopolitical shifts. This is leading to an inflection point in many industries, in the economy and in everyone's personal professional life.  The world is now moving faster than ever — but too many individuals and companies struggle to bridge the gap between the status quo and the future, between vision and execution.  - VCs see brilliant ideas but worry about scalability. - CTOs see the technical opportunities, but manage fragmented teams and legacy systems  - Developers hope for automation of tedious tasks, but still fight with manual inefficiencies - Individuals are excited about the prospects with AI, but worry about their personal professional future  At this point I realized that technology appears linear only in the micro scope, but it is highly disruptive and exponential in the macro scope. This is the reason for these gaps we currently experience in the industry, the economy and in our personal lives  I solve these gaps.  Now, as Founder of MindVenture, I build:  AI Agents that automate repetitive workflows for SMEs, freeing time and reducing errors Blockchain-based Asset Managers that securely handle digital assets, certificates, and smart contracts  I know firsthand that great tech isn’t about hype — it's about building usable, scalable, real-world solutions.  📩 If you are a VC, a CTO, or a tech builder who believes in smart automation, AI, and decentralized ownership, let’s talk.  Together, we can shape what the next decade looks like. Lets talk: https://calendly.com/mindventure

After suffering a data breach, in 2021 I co-founded cybersecurity start-up Seedata.io with security veteran Matt Holland.   Our latest product, vishr.ai, does two things:  ✨ automates vishing simulations with AI voice agents. 🎙️ Then, it delivers personalised voice-based coaching to employees - not more videos, but real, engaging conversations.  Security awareness needs more than content. It needs practice.  𝐎𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐨𝐦 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬 𝐚𝐛𝐨𝐮𝐭 𝐦𝐞 𝐲𝐨𝐮 𝐝𝐨𝐧’𝐭 𝐧𝐞𝐞𝐝 𝐭𝐨 𝐤𝐧𝐨𝐰  * Co-authored "Social Engineering 2030" - now on Amazon. Foreword by Frank Abagnale (yes, the "Catch Me If You Can" guy).  * Came 3rd at DEFCON “Battle of the Bots” in Vegas; our AI bots had to (ethically) coax info by phone from personnel of a large US retailer (yes, this is a thing, and no, there are no recordings...).  *Left Italy when I was 19. Great track record in moving to new countries just before major financial/political crisis (including Ireland, Spain, and UK).  *Launched (& shut) traveltech start-up YouGuide.me. Most common VC comment when trying to fundraise “Execution is key… but guys, this idea is s**t”. Learnt sometimes even VCs are right.  *Turned down an offer to join Revolut in Aug ’15. Yes, that still hurts.  *Spent endless hours wondering around with my metal detector (no, I’ve not found that Treasure JUST YET)  *Did trials with Inter FC & Fiorentina as a goalkeeper… You can guess how those went. about: Before anything: my only, sole and single purpose is to make humanity move forward.  Hey, I am Tom 👋   As you might see on my LinkedIn, I like to code and have some experience with it. Besides coding and solving complex problems, I focus on being the best leader a team needs. I believe in strong independence from each team member, while being animated by a common desire, our mission, and a common direction, our success.  I have been raised as an engineer, and as far as I hate going down the slippery slope of ready-made sentences, I believe in the fact that, as an engineer, "impossible" should not be part of my vocabulary; there are always solutions, I just need to figure out what they are.  I started coding at 14, delved in maths, physics and computer science during high school and post-graduate after joining School 42. I have grown up inside of my father's company and got my firsts own ventures at 16 as a Web developer. I kept doing that for 3 years on my free time, before finishing my common core at 42. I then went on Option E at ESCP to study the entrepreneurship ecosystem and learn how to build a startup.  I strongly believe in the social aspect of coding and building projects, which are unfortunately often overlooked by most developers. "We are safe from AI, clients would need to be able to clearly express their desires" a developer said; I think that a good developer must be as able to express his process and understand his clients as to code.  Well, that's enough talk, let's work together!

about: In life, the more you work hard, the luckier you will become. Others can drive for you, but they cannot walk for you; they can do things for you, but they cannot feel for you. Only when you have determined your direction and run at full speed will the world yield the way for you. Failure is inevitably part of the road to success. Those who are not afraid of failure will succeed, while those who fear failure will fail even more. Do not let the chasing ship be moored in the imaginary port, do not let the path of the battle be filled with thorns... If you choose the sea, ride the wind and waves, spread your wings and fly into the azure sky, be confident! Even if you lose, do not lose your ambition; even if you win, do not lose your calm. Thinking too seriously about winning and losing will make you very tired. Fight lightly, bravely stand up and smile after falling, and start over again. Stick to your original dreams, set sail, ride the wind and waves, and never give up. As long as you are running, the world will run with you; but as long as you stop, the world will leave you behind and run alone. Determine the direction and run at full speed, and the world will surely make way for you.

headline: 10 years of #Tech4Good; Bridging the gender digital divide through GirlsWhoML | Risk at Deloitte | University of Oxford | University of Delhi
about: I am an action-oriented professional, fuelled by a passion for public good. I have shaped my career path from newsrooms to social enterprises and, most recently, technology risk consulting. I also run GirlsWhoML, where we are building an ecosystem to enable more into machine learning and artificial intelligence.  Academically, I read MSc in Social Sciences of the Internet at the University of Oxford's Internet Institute, where I delved into topical issues of online harms, disinformation, and digital inclusion. Here, I was commissioned to work for the UK Home Office to research divisive narratives in the UK information ecosystem.  My dissertation investigated the Government of India’s blanket ban on TikTok, following the India-China cross-border tensions in 2020. My coursework included global internet policy, the evolution of the internet, digital government, internet regulation, AI and ethics of information, media in the global South, and, more broadly, theories relating to power, media and communications and technology. My work experience ranges from civic engagement platforms in India to an international risk rating firm building the world’s first global disinformation risk rankings. I truly believe in the power of technology in fostering positive social change, and for those reasons, I advocate for policies and products that enable access, trust, and a safer and cleaner digital public sphere. I am an optimistic changemaker with an enterprising streak to find possibilities in all situations.  I see technology as an enabler, and I am happy to talk about digital engagement, life @ OII, women's role in grassroots movements in India, and GirlsWhoML.

headline: Stop pasting prompts. Build email-based AI workflows that actually run things. Yes, even your service business. Architect of multi-agent systems for founders stuck inside the companies they built.
about: You built a business that can't run without you.  Every deliverable goes through your hands. Every client depends on your brain. If someone dares you to try to take a week off, the whole thing grinds to a halt.  You already tried the obvious stuff.  Hired people. Burned through cash and spent more time managing than doing the work you actually care about.  Tried AI tools. The output was robotic and you'd spend 3 hours fixing what should've taken 30 minutes. Maybe worse, you lost trust in the whole idea of AI after that.  The problem isn't the tools though.  It's that you ARE the delivery system. And until you replace yourself in that role, nothing changes. Not the hours. Not the stress. Not the ceiling on what you can build next.  I was in the exact same spot. 2 years of 80-hour weeks, manual admin on every project, every "shortcut" just creating another management layer I didn't want.  What actually broke the cycle: I stopped trying to automate tasks and started engineering a replacement for myself entirely.  I spent 18 months building AI teams. Not chatbots. Not prompt templates. Multi-agent systems, where specialized AI workers take on real client fulfillment. They research. They write. They produce actual deliverables, and they check each other's work before anything goes out.  Nothing goes out the door without me seeing it first.  I went from 80-hour weeks to running the business in about 16 hours. Same clients, same quality, but no team to manage and no burnout. That's not a productivity hack. I stopped operating and started actually owning what I built.  Now I'm documenting exactly how I engineer these systems here: → The prompt architecture → The safety nets that catch errors → What breaks in production and how I fix it  If you're a founder stuck inside the business you built, follow along. I share the real process. The ugly parts too.  Or just DM me. I'll walk you through how the system works.

headline: Founder & President @ START Paris | VC Scout | Ambassador @ Lovable | Mentor & Speaker | Empowering the next generation of founders & leaders across Paris & Europe
about: My Mission  I build bridges inside the European startup ecosystem so founders move faster than the system around them. Through START Paris, I challenge the French ecosystem with a global perspective, pushing ambition beyond local habits.   Over the last two years I’ve helped 1,500+ founders, students, and ecosystem leaders access European conferences, investors, and network, but this is only the beginning.   My goal is to align Europe’s fragmented ecosystems into a coherent pathway for founders, investors, and builders.  What I Do Today I lead START Paris to open France to international standards and networks. We design events, delegations, and programs that connect young founders with investors, operators, and global opportunities.   I have mentored startups from Korea, helping them navigate Europe and adapt to new markets, and I supported initiatives such as the launch of EWOR in Paris to broaden pathways into entrepreneurship.  Alongside this, I act as a VC Scout at Growth & Glory and a Lovable Ambassador, helping founders prototype faster and think globally from day one.   I have also contributed as a one-off speaker at the Founder Harbour Summit in Hamburg, speaking about the startup European ecosystem and as a moderator at Space AI Voice Agents, engaging in conversations at the intersection of AI and entrepreneurship.  How I Operate Ecosystems are built by people who show up consistently. I organize initiatives that bring together deeptech founders, students, and investors from across Europe, turning Paris into a meeting point rather than a comfort zone. I also like to have calls & meetings rather than texting  My current priority is to make START Paris sustainable and able to thrive without me, with strong teams, playbooks, and long-term partners in place.  Who I Am French-Danish with years spent in the US, I navigate naturally between cultures and networks. I see Europe as one playground and believe founders should think that way from the start.  Looking Forward Beyond START Paris, I’m exploring new directions to expand impact across Europe. Ideas are forming, and the next chapter is just beginning, stay tuned.  If you’re building, investing, or rethinking how ecosystems should work let’s connect.

headline: Product Designer | AI-Powered Products | Human-in-the-Loop Systems | Complex Workflows
about: I’m a Product Designer working on AI-powered and data-rich products. My work focuses on a recurring product challenge: how to turn complex signals into clear decisions for people.  Across different projects, I’ve worked on problems that involve collecting, interpreting, and prioritising information. The domains vary, but the underlying pattern is the same: translating messy real-world inputs into structured systems people can actually use.  Examples of the types of problems I work on include: • simplifying environmental data collection in a citizen-science app • designing visual systems that help interpret complex weather signals for surfers • exploring AI-assisted coordination tools that structure everyday family logistics.  These projects sit at the intersection of data capture, signal visualisation, and decision-support design. ---  What I work on  • AI-assisted and human-in-the-loop systems: Designing interfaces where automation and human judgment complement each other.  • Data capture and signal interpretation Reducing friction when people collect, classify, or interpret complex information.  • Decision-support experiences Designing products that help people navigate multiple inputs without overwhelming them. ---  How I work  I enjoy working in small product teams where designers collaborate closely with engineers and product stakeholders to shape solutions early.  My process usually combines:  * systems thinking * prototyping and experimentation * user research and usability testing * clear interaction and interface design.  I’m particularly interested in products that interpret environmental, behavioural, or operational signals and turn them into useful insights for people.

about: Hi, my name is Mike. I make AI simple & help businesses use it wisely.  My goal is to help business owners and entrepreneurs capitalize on AI by designing custom solutions with 1-on-1 collaboration.   By wisely introducing AI into your company, you will effectively place yourself ahead of all those who remain unaware of the new paradigm following the rise of ChatGPT.  Whether or not you're familiar with AI automation, you've likely wondered, "What's the point of any of this? How does it directly affect businesses and entrepreneurs?"  Well, many businesses that continue to focus on manual operations open themselves up to a massive blindspot—the limits of human scalability. To remedy this issue, I'll work with you to "inject" AI into strategic positions within your business so you can reserve human employees for only the most vital tasks. If you decide to grow with AI, prepare to:  → Employ custom systems with superhuman efficiency → Save time, money, and stress by automating repetitive tasks → Boost client and lead satisfaction with round-the-clock support and communication  → Optimize internal productivity for management, owners, and employees  → Effortlessly process data, manage projects, and communicate with clients → Master content distribution & power up your marketing processes  If you're like most businesses, you're overwhelmed by AI and reluctant to dive in, yet you nonetheless see what processes and issues continue to consume your precious time.   It doesn’t have to be this way, as my intention is to create fully customized AI solutions that transform the way businesses work.  Here’s My 5-Step Strategy:  1. Evaluate your current workflows. 2. Uncover any pain points or inefficiencies. 3. Determine if AI can fill the role. 4. Distill the task into clear steps. 5. Design you a custom automated solution(s).  More employees or overtime hours will not achieve what I aim to accomplish for you.   I'll do my best to make AI as simple and easy for you as possible—rest assured that you can leave all the technical work to me. Here's what you can expect:  → 1-on-1 consulting and direct collaboration  → A custom set of reliable, unique AI systems with no usage limits → Continued support for ongoing growth and improvement  → Integration of the most cutting-edge GPT and Claude models  You're one message away from making this happen. Reach out directly to me, and we'll make sure you join the top 1% of business owners looking to capitalize on this opportunity.

headline: Turning Skool Communities into ACTUAL 6-7 figures businesses by doubling their MRR every quarter through proprietary frameworks and systems.
about: In 2020, while in IT high school and managing a clothing brand, I immersed myself in NFTs and Web3. Noticing that beginners, celebrities, and companies lacked guidance entering the NFT space, I founded NFTpreneurial—one of the first NFT agencies—to bridge that gap by providing comprehensive support and handling industry complexities.  Without prior experience, I began creating content to build a personal brand on TikTok, which quickly connected me with Web3 creators, builders, and potential clients. With their support, I launched NFTpreneurial in September 2021. During the bull market peak, we collaborated with prominent names in Web2 and Web3, expanding our team week after week.  By October 2022, NFTpreneurial operated autonomously, freeing me to explore new horizons. Over the next year, I invested in myself, learning new skills to tap into emerging markets. Embracing the digital nomad lifestyle in November 2023, I traveled and connected with inspiring entrepreneurs. This journey led me to co-found Skool Quest with Jake and Harry—a company in the creator industry.  Recognizing the challenge of balancing content creation with business growth, I aimed to empower creators. Having launched 8-9 figure crypto communities for years, I was determined to free creators from the constraints of traditional agencies and greedy growth operators. We implemented proven business frameworks—used by 9-figure companies—into the creator industry.  We successfully built and scaled a creator's brand from scratch using our proprietary frameworks within a few months, then equipped him to operate it independently on both business and content fronts. This success validated our frameworks, so we packaged them into a program accessible to all creators. Focusing on the value and effectiveness of our approach, our solutions enable creators to transform their communities—specifically on Skool—into thriving six to seven-figure businesses by doubling their monthly recurring revenue every quarter.

headline: Building What’s Next | Entrepreneurship, Institutions & Geopolitics in Europe | UoL-LSE-Columbia alumnus
about: I work at the intersection of entrepreneurship, institutional strategy, and geopolitics, with the conviction that policy and venture-building must operate as one integrated ecosystem. My focus is not simply on analysing Europe’s strategic challenges, but on building structures that align markets, institutions, and emerging actors. In an era defined by fragmentation and geopolitical competition, I believe Europe’s resilience will depend on its ability to merge entrepreneurial execution with institutional depth.  In the policy sphere, I serve as Head of Partnerships at VOYCE (Voice of Youth for Change in Europe), where I built and now lead a 15-member team responsible for external engagement across 60+ universities. I co-authored the flagship proposal, European Partnership for Democratic Resilience, on credible EU enlargement and led its project coordination through finalisation. My work has also included structuring multinational delegations, contributing to research on democratic legitimacy and strategic autonomy, and engaging in multilateral processes such as the UN SB62 negotiations.  Beyond individual initiatives, I have coordinated multi-country Erasmus+ proposals across European and Mediterranean partners, translating political priorities into operational, fundable frameworks.  Entrepreneurship is not separate from this work; it is central to it. Through startup and accelerator environments, I explore how new ventures can serve as testing grounds for systemic innovation: faster execution, better coordination, and more adaptive models of governance-market interaction. My experience in competitive venture settings, including placing 3rd out of 400+ teams in Accenture's In Real Life Challenge, reinforced my belief that ecosystem alignment is the decisive variable in both markets and institutions.  My publications examine EU enlargement (Georgia, Serbia & Kosovo), Europe's soft power posture in Africa, and the structural pressures facing the European project. Across research and execution, my central concern remains consistent: how can Europe design adaptive ecosystems capable of converting strategic ambition into durable outcomes?  I studied International Relations at the University of London & LSE, to then pursue executive training at Columbia University and Genoa Entrepreneurship School. Fluent in English and French, and currently learning Italian, I operate comfortably across borders, sectors, and institutional cultures.

headline: Building something new · Research at Inria, Lectures at ENS/MVA · Ex: Llama at GenAI, Meta; Gemini and BYOL at Google DeepMind
about: Michal is a founding researcher at a stealth AI startup, tenured researcher at Inria, and the lecturer at the MVA master of ENS Paris-Saclay. Michal is primarily interested in designing algorithms that would require as little human supervision as possible. He is working on methods and settings that are able to deal with minimal feedback, such as deep reinforcement learning, bandit algorithms, self-supervised learning, or self play. Michal has recently worked on representation learning, word models and deep (reinforcement) learning algorithms that have some theoretical underpinning. In the past he worked on sequential algorithms with structured decisions where exploiting the structure leads to provably faster learning. Michal is now working on large large models (LMMs), in particular providing algorithmic solutions for their scalable fine-tuning and alignment. He received his Ph.D. in 2011 from the University of Pittsburgh, before getting a tenure at Inria in 2012 and co-creating Google DeepMind Paris with Rémi Munos. In 2024, he became the principal Llama engineer at Meta, building online reinforcement learning stack and research for Llama 3.

headline: Founder & CEO, Vabble.io | Buyers confirm before suppliers get paid | Institutional receivables infrastructure | NED, NASDAQ: ALCY | Open to aligned investors
about: Hi, I'm Pablo. My journey from an Argentine farm to founding vabble.io is rooted in personal experience and global finance expertise. Growing up, I witnessed my father's struggles with delayed payments for his crops. This personal experience, combined with the knowledge and relationships accumulated during my career in New York and London, inspired me to create a solution.  vabble.io connects suppliers' assets with investors using cutting-edge technology. We leverage automation and machine learning to streamline trade finance payments, enabling businesses to access funds quickly and efficiently. Our platform is backed by leading institutional investors providing liquidity in an entirely new customer-friendly take on an old business—factoring—advancing how producers and suppliers manage their export-related cash flows.  My working experience took me across Buenos Aires, São Paulo, Santiago, Lima, Madrid, Hamburg, Geneva, New York, Singapore, Bangkok, and London, giving me a unique global perspective on business practices. For 20+ years, I've specialized in what I call "continental connecting"—authentically operating across geographic, cultural, and institutional boundaries in global finance.  My career spans:  Investment banking at Goldman Sachs and UBS Asset management at UBS and Banco Santander Suisse Corporate finance at Klesch, Knightsbridge, and PACT-AM Commercial banking at Banco Francés BBVA and Standard Chartered  Today, I'm passionate about harnessing technology to solve real-world problems, especially in emerging markets where the impact can yield amazing results. As part of a network supporting Latin American startups, I'm committed to fostering innovation in the region.  I believe in transparency, diversity, creativity, and always doing the right thing. Inspired by Peter Drucker's principle that "culture eats strategy for breakfast," this guides everything we do at Vabble.io. Our guiding principle is "long-term greedy," and we are building a powerful financial inclusion tool in the B2B space.

headline: Building Looply — AI that works every lead until it converts
about: Most companies don’t actually have a lead problem.  They have a **conversion problem**.  Leads come in from ads, websites, referrals, DMs, outreach… but inside most businesses the reality looks like this:  • slow responses • missed follow-ups • overwhelmed sales teams • opportunities slipping through the cracks  Good prospects simply disappear.  That’s the problem I focus on.  I’m building **Looply**, an AI system designed to work every lead the way a great salesperson would — researching prospects, engaging across channels, following up intelligently, handling objections, and pushing deals forward until a clear outcome happens.  The goal is simple:  Turn more of the leads businesses already have into actual customers.  So sales teams spend their time **closing real opportunities**, not chasing cold or forgotten leads.  If your company generates leads but feels like revenue is leaking somewhere in the process — that’s exactly what Looply is built to solve.  Always open to connecting with founders, operators, agencies, and teams thinking about how AI can improve real business outcomes.

currentTitle: Researcher / Designer ⸱ Strategic Systems: Brand, Service, Interaction
headline: strategic research + design ⸱ ux/ui design engineer ⸱ systems, tools / experiences, behaviour
about: Hello!  Amongst all this information, competition for, and commodification of our attention, my focus is to help make product and service systems truly resonate with and support the people they touch: providing real, lasting satisfaction and creating meaningful value.  Essentially, I love to appreciate and sift through the complexity of our world: helping to navigate its possibilities towards emergent opportunities.  I help my clients explore their customers' experiences as well as their own, exploring the underlying psychology, cultures, patterns and behaviours. We then use that information to design brand strategies, products and services (experiences) that offer meaningful value (quality).   🔍 What I Do  I’m a research-driven designer and create tailored approaches that draw from a broad range of qualitative and quantitative methods.  Fields I often draw from:  + (Micro-)phenomenology, ethnography and co-creation + Market/cultural research and psychographics + Semiotics and communication theory + Cognitive science (eg. Gestalt psychology, psychophysics, neuroaesthetics, environmental psychology) + Behavioural science (adapting choice architecture and ‘nudging’ individuals to foster behaviour change and help them achieve their goals) + Cybernetics and systems thinking/doing  I specialise in strategic thinking and digital product design (UX/UI and web development) and have versatile creative skills across graphics (including layout, motion, 3D and data visualisation) and product/spatial design (CAD, game engines and 3D rendering) that help us make things real.   🛠️ Real-world Applications  + Nudging shoppers toward more sustainable choices, in collaboration with a top UK supermarket. + An inclusively-designed circadian lighting system, supporting ageing societies and dementia care. + A minimal impact thermal bath house to foster individual, social + ecological wellbeing. + Prototyping ways of cooking for everyday regenerative action.   🌏 Design for Wellbeing  Design plays a beautiful role in the world when it effectively bridges organisational strategy with individual goals and planetary needs. That’s where I focus — working with those who want to make tomorrow better than today.  I’m particularly fascinated in perception and behaviour, with a focus on cross-cultural (and -species) perception and design for wellbeing.  I'm always looking for ways to foster delight and, as Naoto Fukasawa puts it, design in a way that 'dissolves into behaviour'.   📞 Let's Connect  Always keen for a chat, errant wander or tea. Do get in touch!  hello[at]algy.design

headline: 2x SWE Intern @ Uber || Fellow @ EuroTech Federation || CSE BSc @ TU Delft
about: 👋 About Me  I didn’t always imagine myself as a software engineer (as a kid I actually wanted to be an athlete…). What first sparked my interest was watching my older brother build all sorts of cool things with computers and electronics when we were kids. I always wanted to join in, and that curiosity eventually led me to take IT courses in high school. That’s where I discovered competitive programming, which I ended up doing for three years - even making it to the Polish Olympiad in Informatics finals.  The funny part? Somewhere along the way, I realized I could solve very tough algorithmic problems but had no idea how to build something practical, like a website or an app. That’s when it hit me: I wanted to be more than “good at coding.” I wanted to become a real SWE.   💼 Experience  When I got to TU Delft, I realized the best way to learn was by actually doing. After my first year, I jumped into my first internship, and it turned out to be one of the best choices I’ve made. During these 3 months I've learned more than in my past year at uni.  That's why I’m soon-to-be on my 5th internship, and what I enjoy most is how different each one has been. From big tech to finance, from frontend to backend - each role has given me a new perspective on what it means to be a SWE.   💡 Why SWE  At the heart of it, what I love most is problem-solving. It’s the same thing that made me enjoy competitive programming, but now the puzzles are tied to real people and real systems. I get to think not just about solving the problem, but about designing a solution that others will actually use and benefit from.  I especially enjoy the design side of SWE: figuring out how to make an interface reusable, scale a system, and balance tradeoffs. All in all, I just enjoy figuring out solutions that feel both practical and elegant.

Don't think I forgot you copywriters,  I will also give out tips, you need for being a better writer by giving you big ideas, critiquing copy and overall turning you into an expert writing salesman.  So if that's something you'd want, copywriters and businesses  DM or book a call with me and I'll see if I can turn your business into a money dripper on autopilot.  Take care everyone, see you soon. headline: Founder @ Outlyst.ai | Helping B2B Companies Start More Conversations
about: I help businesses scale. That’s a bold claim… but I stand by it.  I’ve worked with B2B companies across multiple sectors – ranging from fintech startups and management consultancies to marketing agencies, cleaning services, and even niche mobile network solutions providers. The goal is always the same: help them reach the right people, more consistently, and with less manual effort.  Most teams were relying on outdated processes. Cold emails sent manually, inconsistent follow-ups, and little to no system in place. I started building workflows to fix that: cleaner data, better targeting, and messaging that actually connects.  At one point, I was managing outreach for 12 companies at the same time. That pace pushed me to build smarter systems. As things scaled, I trained one person to help carry the load. That quickly turned into a team of four. Outlyst.ai was born from the need to professionalize what I had already been doing: helping B2B companies automate outbound outreach using AI and no-code tools like Clay, Zapier, and Make.  At Outlyst, we build systems that do what 10-20 BDRs would traditionally handle – at a fraction of the cost. Our AI-driven outbound workflows efficiently identify high-value prospects, deliver hyper-personalized messaging, and systematically follow up. This is without the high costs and inefficiencies typical of manual sales teams. Our clients are able to focus on closing deals, knowing their outreach is reliably automated, personalized, and efficient.  In 2024, our systems helped generate over 800 qualified replies and meetings across the companies we worked with.  I’m 20 years old, currently pursuing a Bachelor's degree in Business Data Analytics, a field that merges my interest in business strategy with data-driven decision making. I’ve always seen potential at the intersection of tech, data, and sales. And that’s where I aim to build!

headline: Helping 1000 Polish SMBs apply AI & Automation by 2029  | AI Mentorship/Consulting/Training | n8n & Automation
about: Czy nadal tracisz czas na ręczne, powtarzalne zadania? Czy słyszysz o AI i automatyzacji, ale nie wiesz, jak je zastosować w praktyce? Czy boisz się, że Twoja konkurencja właśnie buduje przewagę… a Ty stoisz w miejscu?  Nie musisz wszystkiego ogarniać samodzielnie.  Pomagam zespołom, freelancerom i właścicielom firm budować realne rozwiązania z AI i automatyzacją (nawet jeśli dopiero zaczynają).  Robię to na 2 sposoby:  1. Szkolenia i warsztaty z automatyzacji i agentów AI (dla zespołów i klientów indywidualnych) 2. Konsulting i mentoring 1:1 — jeśli chcesz rozwijać się szybciej lub wdrożyć AI w konkretne procesy  Nie uczę “jak kliknąć”. Uczę jak myśleć, rozumieć i samodzielnie budować.  Na moich szkoleniach i sesjach: ✔ budujesz pierwsze automatyzacje, które naprawdę działają ✔ nie kopiujesz gotowców, lecz rozumiesz, co i dlaczego działa ✔ zyskujesz pewność, że AI nie musi być trudne, ani magiczne ✔ przełamujesz strach przed technologią i zaczynasz budować z odwagą ✔ uczysz się wykorzystywać AI… do nauki i rozwiązywania problemów ✔ uczysz się mentalnych modeli, które pasują do każdego narzędzia  A jeśli wolisz na razie nie szkolić zespołu, mogę wesprzeć Cię jako doradca.  Oto przykładowe automatyzacje i AI-asystenci, które pomogły moim klientom zaoszczędzić dziesiątki godzin: ✔ automatyczne przepisywanie danych z faktur, zdjęć, rozmów, maili do CRM ✔ podsumowania spotkań z zadaniami, follow-upami i gotowymi mailami ✔ asystenci AI tworzący oferty, raporty z researchu lub opisy produktów ✔ narzędzia wewnętrzne do zarządzania wiedzą i komunikacją zespołu  Bez kodu. Bez chaosu. Bez wstydu, że „czegoś nie wiesz”.  Jeśli Twoja firma lub Twój zespół jest na etapie "chcemy, ale nie wiemy jak", porozmawiajmy. Pokażę Ci, że AI to nie magia... AI to logika, którą da się zrozumieć.  Pewnie masz jeszcze pełno pytań i wątpliwości…  Porozmawiajmy! Ja chętnie wszystko wyjaśnię. headline: Bayside Integrations | We help growing companies implement AI to scale without hiring staff | Book a call below & see how AI can be implemented into your business!
about: AI isn’t the future anymore—it’s the present, and businesses that don’t adapt will be left behind. Everybody knows how to use ChatGPT, but few know how to integrate AI into their business like a full-time employee—automating workflows & completing tasks better and faster & cheaper than any human on the planet. That's where I come in.  I help small businesses and agencies integrate AI systems seamlessly into their day to day workflows, completely replacing in house hiring, and unlocking new levels of revenue growth. As a top 1% Upwork freelancer specializing in AI Integrations, I've devoted the better part of the last year figuring out how to replace workers with AI machines.  With a proven track record of driving results, we've:  Built an AI Automated lead gen cold email system, generating 128 meetings per month with ideal leads, without lifting a finger.  Developed AI customer support chat & voice systems saving 11k per month & a 70% (estimated) increase in customer satisfaction.  Automated CRM enrichment leading to $65,000+ in annual savings for clients like SEMrush.   Streamlined CRM processes by integrating AI note-takers that match customer profiles with the right salesperson, ensuring timely and personalized follow-ups.  Built systems that automate content publishing across 7 main social media platforms to edit, add captions, and transcribe both long-form and short-form content on autopilot.  Integrated voice AI agents into businesses for cold calling, replacing multiple SDR’s  Our expertise goes beyond traditional automation — automating entire accounting workflows (AP/AR), contract management, web scraping, and even load underwriting processes. Whether it’s integrating AI note-takers into CRM workflows or using custom APIs to automate complex business tasks, he dedicates himself to designing systems that scale.  My quest for drive started at a young age, starting skiing at age 2, & managed to climb up the ranks and become one of the top-ranked freestyle skiers in the country for my age. On route to qualifying for Jr World Championships, I tore my ACL & meniscus, ending a childhood career and dream. After surgery, I began working on small startups, where I found AI.  With a true driven passion for AI, automation, and entrepreneurship, I constantly push the boundaries of what’s possible in business automation. If you’re looking to streamline your operations, increase efficiency, and unlock new growth opportunities, let’s connect.  Contact  📧 Luke@baysideintegrations.com 🌐 https://baysideintegrations.com

headline: Founder | AI Product Manager
about: I’m driven by helping people flourish, and by fulfilling my own potential in the pursuit of that. Since moving into tech, my work has centred on two themes: improving physical and mental health (currently at Joy), and accelerating paths to self-actualisation (through products I’ve built like Nudge and Converge). I care about moving people up Maslow’s hierarchy in practical, measurable ways.  Nudge was a group-based habit tracker built to pull people back into the real world. Much of our biology hasn’t caught up with modern incentive systems, and behaviour-shaping technology often exploits that gap. I’m interested in building tools that work with human nature, not against it. I'll be working on something like that again soon.  I’m guided by a small set of operating principles:  - integrity: alignment between thought, action, and communication (feels like knowing god) - pursuit of excellence: life is too beautiful to half arse it - passion: only working on problems that feel like part of my life's work - first-principles thinking: especially where leverage is high and assumptions are costly  More on my career:  I have experience across customer discovery & validation; rapid prototyping and mvps; data analytics & metrics design; product marketing & positioning; full-stack development, ai/ml implementation; ux and learning design systems  I built Converge, a career platform that pivoted three times while searching for truth in value creation: B2C discovery → B2C navigation → B2B internal mobility. I stopped when I realised I’d drifted from empowering individuals to serving organisations. Purpose beats traction.  At Joy, I lead product across five technically complex products, working toward a unified operating system for preventative health in the UK. If social prescribing integrates cleanly into clinical workflows (shared notes, credible impact metrics) it stops being an add-on and starts compounding.

headline: Confidential Thinking Partner To Post-Exit Female Founders| Multi-Sector Strategic Intelligence Expert | Investor
about: For many founders, the most complex phase of the journey begins after the exit.  For years, life had a clear structure: build the company, lead the team, grow the enterprise.  Then the liquidity event happens.  Externally, it looks like freedom and success. Internally, a far more nuanced transition begins.  Advisors appear with new opportunities. Family dynamics shift. New ventures, investments, and philanthropic ideas compete for attention.  Yet the deeper questions are often harder to answer:  What should the next chapter actually look like? What decisions truly reflect what I want now? How do I navigate expectations from advisors, family, and the world around me?  And perhaps most importantly:  Where is the place where I can think about these decisions honestly?  I work privately with a small number of post-exit female founders navigating this exact transition.  My role is not financial advice, investment management, or operational consulting.  Instead, I serve as a confidential thinking partner for the decisions where money, identity, family, and legacy are intertwined.  The work centers around conversations such as:  • designing a second act that feels authentic rather than expected • navigating the shift from operator to principal • making capital and life decisions with clarity rather than pressure • aligning wealth, purpose, and personal fulfillment • creating space to think without performance or obligation  Because of the depth and confidentiality involved, I limit this work to a very small number of principals each year.  The twelve-month engagement is called The Principal Year.  It is designed to support founders as they move from the intensity of building a company into the responsibility—and freedom—of living as a principal.  If you are navigating life after an exit and value a space where you can think clearly and honestly about the decisions ahead, I welcome a private conversation.
topSkills: Female Entrepreneurship, Financial Services, Authority Building, AI Powered Marketing, Business Development

headline: ✍  Helping content creators make money online - AI Creator Monetization Lab
about: A decade at Microsoft ignited my passion for technology, leading me to an entrepreneurial path. Alongside two colleagues, I built pioneering software for online testing, culminating in a seven-figure exit after seven years. Despite success, heavy investment diluted my share enormously, and I vowed never to make the same mistake again.  While running this venture, I founded a web design and content marketing agency, blending technical and marketing skills to create and sell innovative solutions. After seven years, I sought new challenges and launched a content agency to help creators monetise their passions. Rapid growth saw us expand to a team of 140+, poised for a high seven-figure exit, until AI emerged.  AI's rise decimated our business, wiping out 80% overnight. Burdened by UK regulations, we couldn't downsize quickly enough to survive. Nearing a sale that would have been 'retirement money,' this was hard to take.   I took a year to pursue personal passions and strategise my next mission. My career's constant theme has been about helping business success, and now, AI and automation present unparalleled opportunities. As I type, my computer chimes at me to let me know that it’s just completed an automated task in seconds that would have previously taken me hours to do manually. This is the transformative power of AI.  With a wealth of experience in technology, I'm eager to leverage AI and automation, empowering businesses and individuals to focus on the things they love and automate the rest.

headline: Building Pinged - Find customers for your product without hours of manual searching.
about: I’ve never been the naturally gifted kid. Not in Olympiads, not in Abacus, not in design.  As a child, I tried everything: painting, drawing, violin, sports, Science Olympiad, Cyber Olympiad, Math Olympiad. I failed a lot. 7th → 5th → 2nd → 3rd → 4th → nd again.  But one thing stayed with me: I didn’t quit.  Eventually, I earned gold medals in Math and GK Olympiads 🏅 simply because I kept showing up. Same with Abacus: I finished 8 levels, entered Grandmasters 🎓, was the slowest kid there, and still finished.  That taught me something important: I don’t start strong. I become strong because I stay in the game.  🎨 Rediscovering the Creative Kid  Bodybuilding taught me discipline. Engineering taught me what does not excite me. Design showed me what does.  UI/UX felt like reconnecting with my creative side. My early designs were bad, but posting them anyway helped me grow fast. I learned, built, taught juniors, hosted bootcamps and found communities that shaped how I think.  ✈️ Moving to Bangalore  After graduating, I moved to Bangalore for my first onsite job. The best part was not the job. It was the people.  I met some of the smartest and most driven people I know. Instead of insecurity, it became fuel. That circle became one of the biggest gifts of my journey.  👉 Career Highlights  I have designed for early-stage startups and turned vague ideas into intuitive MVPs:  🔹 Banzan (Snackr) redesigned a raw MVP (B2B and B2C) improving onboarding, creator flows and retention. Featured at IGDC 2025. 🔹 Unacademy (Airlearn) designed multilingual lessons and created contextual Midjourney visuals 🎨 to improve clarity. 🔹 Ostello built AI-driven MCQs, summaries, practice flows and demo nudges that improved trial conversions. 🔹 Finance Mithra designed a voice-assisted kiosk for rural low-literacy users, reducing task time by 90 percent. 🔹 HealthPro (Current) designing medical CRMs and OI Charts, a strategy builder tool. Landing pages achieved 67 to 70 percent conversion.  ⭐ Where I Am Now  I experiment, learn, break things, fix them, repeat.  I am building StickyFounder with my close friend, documenting our early founder journey, lessons, mistakes and tools that actually solve problems.  I do not know everything about startups yet, but I know this: You don’t get better by waiting. You get better by trying.  What I Believe ✨  Your first version will not be perfect. Your tenth might still be rough. But if you keep going, something eventually clicks.  I am here to design, build, fail, learn and show up every day.

If you’ve answered YES – then read on…  Here at Impactful Marketing we are FB Ad lead generation experts who specialises in filling lists, events, webinars and challenges with thousands of high-quality cost-effective leads.  Maybe you already run FB Ads for your business but struggle to obtain large numbers of high-quality cost-effective leads?  Perhaps you don’t already run FB Ads but you know you need more high-quality cost-effective leads in order to grow your business and increase your impact on the world? But this also makes them harder and more expensive to find and acquire.  And last but not least, the cost. Being able to keep your cost per lead low when trying to get large numbers of high-quality leads is a challenge.   And to achieve this utilizing the ever-evolving FB Ads platform, without spending a lot of money on mistakes and a lot of time creating and managing the ads, is a no mean feat. headline: Founder of Morningside AI, AAA Accelerator & Agentive | AI Educator & Creator of the AI Automation Agency Model
about: Aside from working at a Pizza shop for a few months, I've been fortunate enough to be an entrepreneur my entire adult life and achieve – some time ago – my dream of working on projects that excite me, with people I love being around, from anywhere in the world.  My entrepreneurial journey kicked off during my gap year in Edinburgh as a way to prove to myself that I could translate my high school success or ‘book smarts’ into ‘real world’ smarts, which to me was being able to make money and not be painfully broke.   This decision launched me into the world of ecommerce where over 3 years I got the best business crash course a fledging entrepreneur could ask for, dropping out of my business degree along the way. While running my online stores I cut my teeth in the digital marketing space, an invaluable skillset that has been foundational to my “long term” success (I’m 24, only time will tell!).  As the ecommerce headwinds of 2020 battered my business, my mentor Josh Brown offered me a position to join his digital marketing agency JB Media, where I further developed my skillset.  In 2022, I was able to circle back to on an old passion of mine that had been left by the wayside in my pursuit of entrepreneurial success: coding.  Over the next 12 months I taught myself Python specializing in Machine Learning and wrapped up 2022 with my first exposure to language models such as BERT for sentiment analysis tasks. It was at this time I first tried ChatGPT and instantly knew that this was where my focus would be for the coming years.  To dive in head first, I launched my YouTube channel on January 3rd 2023, starting a new chapter in my entrepreneurial journey. My rapid growth led me to founding my own AI Development company Morningside AI, named after the suburb in New Zealand I grew up in.  Since then, I have continued to create content helping entrepreneurs wrap their heads around AI, reaching 570,000 subs as of writing (Jun 2025). This audience allowed me to launch the education arm of Morningside through our flagship product “AAA Accelerator” where we help entrepreneurs to start and scale their own AI Agencies.  What started off as a way to prove to myself that I had ‘real world’ smarts has blossomed into a drive to reach the pinnacle of entrepreneurship by building the world’s leading AI development firm and impact the lives of millions by making AI education accessible to all.  I am incredibly grateful for this opportunity and am also incredibly excited for what is to come.

headline: I help you create the OS that takes your agency from 0→1 | Co-founder of Sawify
about: Over the last 3 years, I've been building AI and automation systems for other businesses.  Then, in late 2024, I hit my own wall.  I was spending 15+ hours a week on LinkedIn. Posting manually. Following up on every lead. Creating content from scratch daily. DMing prospects one by one.  I was getting results, but I was the entire system. Every post required me. Every reply required me. Every lead required me.  I'd built systems for other people's businesses, but I was still running mine on effort and availability.  So I built a system that worked without me. 3 months later, I had 5 hours back. Leads were flowing in without manual outreach. Content was running while I slept.  But something else happened. I stopped feeling like my business could break if I took a day off. Other founders noticed. They kept asking how I did it.  So I turned it into Sawify - a SaaS for LinkedIn content systemization. But as I started working with more founders, I saw the same pattern everywhere.  LinkedIn wasn't the problem. It was just the most visible symptom.  Most founders are brilliant at delivery and terrible at infrastructure. They're capable, but they're the bottleneck. Their business runs on them, not systems.  Now I build operating systems for founders and agency owners. Some need content systemized. Others need sales ops rebuilt. Others need client delivery automated. Some need all of it.  I diagnose what's breaking from manual effort and build the structure that replaces it. Not because systems are impressive. Because businesses that run on effort don't scale. They exhaust.  Sawfy website - sawify.io Substack - @miljkovicaleksandar

headline: Full Stack AI Engineer | LLM Systems | RAG | LangChain | React.js/Next.js/FastAPI | Building Production AI Systems
about: I’ve been spending the last couple of years building AI systems that actually work in the real world.  Most of my work sits around RAG pipelines, and agent systems. Over time, I’ve realised that getting something to “work” is easy, but making it reliable, measurable, and useful in production is where things get interesting.  At the moment, I’m working on AI agents integrated with real business workflows focusing on things like context handling, evaluation, and system reliability. A lot of the improvements I’ve worked on have been around reducing hallucinations, improving response quality, and making these systems behave consistently under real usage.  Alongside that, I’ve also been building FlexyWork AI an early-stage product where we’re using voice, SMS, and AI agents to automate workforce operations. It’s been a great way to think beyond features and really focus on how systems behave in messy, real-world scenarios.  I enjoy working across the stack when needed backend, APIs, infra but I naturally lean towards designing systems, especially where AI meets real-world constraints.  Outside of work, I’ve been quite involved in the AI community in London speaking at events, helping organise meetups, and just spending time with people building similar things. A lot of my thinking has been shaped by these conversations.  Right now, looking for roles where I can work on meaningful AI systems especially in teams that care about building things properly.  If you’re working on something interesting in this space, I’d be happy to connect.

headline: CEO at Blackwood Enterprises | Reliability, Safety Governance & Institutional AI Risk
about: I’m the CEO of Blackwood Enterprises, an operations-focused firm supporting organizations that run safety-critical infrastructure.  We work with hospitals, municipalities, utilities, and industrial operators to strengthen facility reliability, regulatory compliance, and long-term operating performance. Our work typically begins with structured reliability reviews and documentation assessments, followed by corrective execution and ongoing service arrangements where appropriate.  Rather than emphasizing rapid transformation or speculative technology adoption, Blackwood focuses on operational fundamentals: clear accountability, verifiable documentation, disciplined maintenance practices, and systems that remain resilient under regulatory scrutiny.  My background includes independent consulting across operational planning, process documentation, and governance design for organizations facing complex compliance or infrastructure challenges. That work informed the creation of Blackwood as a vehicle for delivering more structured, long-term operational support rather than one-off advisory engagements.  We are building Blackwood deliberately, prioritizing execution capacity, safety oversight, and financial discipline before pursuing scale. Our goal is to be a reliable long-term partner for organizations that cannot afford operational ambiguity.  Recently, I have also been contributing to public-sector work examining the governance implications of artificial intelligence within municipal systems and urban infrastructure. This work focuses on ensuring that emerging technologies are introduced with clear institutional accountability, executive oversight, and operational discipline.  If your organization operates in a regulated or high-consequence environment and needs stronger operational clarity, reliability systems, or documented accountability, I’m always open to conversations.

headline: I help enterprises stop talking about AI and start using it
about: Your firm bought AI tools. You ran the training. Everyone nodded along. Six months later, usage is under 20% — but nobody knows because no one's tracking it. The investment isn't paying off, and it's invisible. This is the gap I fix. Think of it like surgery and physical therapy. The vendor does the implementation. But someone has to make sure the firm actually uses it — that behavior changes, that workflows improve, that the investment pays off. I'm the PT. Most AI consultants sell tools or run training. I come in after — when the training's done and nothing's changed. I measure what matters: not course completions or satisfaction scores, but actual behavior change. Who's using it. How often. For what. And what's getting in the way. This isn't theory. At JPMorgan Chase, I led AI enablement for over 16,000 employees. We hit a 250% lift in engagement. More importantly, the people I trained built 560 automated solutions on their own — without being told to. That's what real adoption looks like. Today I work with law firms, accounting firms, and wealth advisory practices — typically 10 to 200 people — who've already invested in AI and aren't seeing the return. If you're the one responsible for making that investment pay off, we should talk. → Message me or book a conversation: https://cal.com/jason.hess
